dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_5-The organisation's governance structure is described in written documents and is known to the staff of the organisation.

SURV_HOSP_7-There is an organisational chart or document that describes the lines of authority and accountability between the governance structure and the organisation as well as within the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_9-Those responsible for governance approve and make public the organisation's mission statement.
SURV_HOSP_11-Those responsible for governance approve the managerial policies and plans to operate the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_13-Those responsible for governance approve the budget and allocate resources required to meet the organisation's mission.
SURV_HOSP_15-Those responsible for governance appoint the organisation's senior manager(s) or director(s).
SURV_HOSP_17-Those responsible for governance collaborate with the organisation's managers.
SURV_HOSP_19-Those responsible for governance receive and act upon reports of the quality programme at least quarterly.
SURV_HOSP_21-Those responsible for governance receive and act upon reports on risk management at least quarterly.
SURV_HOSP_23-Those responsible for governance evaluate the performance of the organisation's senior manager at least annually.
SURV_HOSP_28-The senior manager manages the day-to-day operation of the organisation, including those responsibilities described in the position description.
SURV_HOSP_30-The senior manager has the education and experience to match the requirements in the position description.
SURV_HOSP_32-The senior manager carries out approved policies for management functions.
SURV_HOSP_34-The senior manager assures compliance with applicable laws and regulations.
SURV_HOSP_36-The senior manager responds to any reports from inspecting and regulatory agencies.
SURV_HOSP_40-The senior manager facilitates communication and cooperation between the organisation's governance structure, management and the community.
SURV_HOSP_42-The senior manager implements processes to manage and control human, financial and other resources.
SURV_HOSP_44-The senior manager ensures that the required physical facilities, installations and equipment are available and are used optimally to provide the specified services.
SURV_HOSP_46-The senior manager ensures the implementation of risk management processes and activities.
SURV_HOSP_48-The senior manager implements processes to monitor patient expectations and satisfaction.
SURV_HOSP_50-The senior manager implements processes to monitor staff expectations and satisfaction.
SURV_HOSP_52-The senior manager implements processes for quality management and improvement.
SURV_HOSP_54-The senior manager implements processes to monitor the quality of clinical and other services.
SURV_HOSP_58-The leaders of the organisation are formally identified.
SURV_HOSP_60-The leaders work collaboratively to develop and implement the strategic plan of the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_62-The organisation's leaders meet with the leaders of other provider organisations in their community to develop and revise strategic and operational plans to meet the community needs.
SURV_HOSP_66-The organisation has identified its target population.
SURV_HOSP_68-The organisation has implemented a communication strategy with these populations.
SURV_HOSP_70-The organisation provides information on the quality of its services.
SURV_HOSP_74-The organisation's leaders establish ethical and legal norms that protect patients and their rights.
SURV_HOSP_76-The organisation discloses its ownership.
SURV_HOSP_78-The organisation honestly portrays its services to patients.
SURV_HOSP_80-The organisation provides clear admission, treatment, transfer and discharge policies.
SURV_HOSP_82-The organisation accurately bills for services.
SURV_HOSP_84-The organisation has a committee or other mechanism to control all research undertaken within the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_88-The organisation's leaders ensure that policies and procedures guide and support the activities and management of the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_90-A designated manager is responsible for compiling and indexing policies and procedures and for ensuring their circulation, recall and review.
SURV_HOSP_92-Policies and procedures are signed by persons authorised to do so.
SURV_HOSP_94-Policies and procedures are compiled into comprehensive manual(s) or a filing system, which is indexed and easily accessible to all personnel.
SURV_HOSP_96-All policies and procedures are reviewed at appropriate intervals, dated and signed.
SURV_HOSP_98-There is a mechanism to ensure that policies are known and implemented.
SURV_HOSP_102-Copies of contracts are made available to those who ensure their implementation.
SURV_HOSP_104-Services provided under contracts/agreements meet patient needs.
SURV_HOSP_106-Contracts and other arrangements are monitored as part of the organisation's quality management and improvement programme.
SURV_HOSP_108-There is a mechanism to ensure that all volunteers work under the guidance of suitably qualified health professionals in the employ of the care centre.
SURV_HOSP_110-Volunteers sign a memorandum of agreement to abide by the conditions of the centre.
SURV_HOSP_112-There are written policies and procedures for the activities of the volunteer service.
SURV_HOSP_116-The organisation's leaders promote communication among departments, services and individual staff members.
SURV_HOSP_118-Agendas are prepared for meetings, and the personnel are given timely notification in order to prepare for participation.
SURV_HOSP_120-Minutes of meetings are taken and are circulated to all relevant personnel.

SURV_HOSP_122-There is a mechanism to ensure that key issues resulting from meetings of the governing body and/or the management of the organisation are communicated to and acted upon by the personnel.
SURV_HOSP_127-The organisation ensures that a qualified individual manages each department or service in the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_129-The responsibilities of each departmental manager are defined in writing.
SURV_HOSP_131-The departmental or service manager implements processes to manage and control human, financial and other resources.
SURV_HOSP_133-The departmental or service manager ensures that there are sufficient personnel to provide the services.
SURV_HOSP_135-The departmental or service manager ensures that resources are available to provide those services.
SURV_HOSP_137-Departmental or service managers provide orientation and training for all personnel of the department or service appropriate to their responsibilities.
SURV_HOSP_139-There is coordination and integration of services with other departments and services.
SURV_HOSP_141-Departmental managers implement quality control and improvement programmes.
SURV_HOSP_145-Care planning and delivery is integrated and co-ordinated among care settings, departments and services.
SURV_HOSP_147-Clinical practice guidelines relevant to the patients and services of the organisation are implemented to guide uniform patient care processes.
SURV_HOSP_149-The hospital maintains a clinical record for each patient.
SURV_HOSP_151-The patients' clinical records are completed according to guidelines determined by the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_157-A designated individual is responsible for providing support for the organisation's human resource strategy.
SURV_HOSP_159-The human resource manager is suitably qualified and experienced in human resource management.
SURV_HOSP_161-The human resource manager ensures that policies and procedures are available to guide the personnel and that they are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_163-The human resource manager uses information on staffing needs provided by clinical and managerial personnel to ensure adequate staff provision.

SURV_HOSP_165-Details of the organisation's absenteeism, sickness rates and staff turnover rates are recorded and analysed, to allow for informed decision making by the management of the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_167-Details of the staff establishment (i.e. available posts, filled and vacant posts) are recorded and analysed to allow for informed decision making by the organisation's management.
SURV_HOSP_169-Receptionists, telephonists, clerical support personnel and porters are allocated to wards and departments in accordance with their needs.
SURV_HOSP_173-A designated member of staff is responsible for the storage and retrieval of personnel records.
SURV_HOSP_175-There is documented personnel information on each staff member.
SURV_HOSP_177-Personnel files are standardised.
SURV_HOSP_179-Personnel files are reviewed at least annually.
SURV_HOSP_181-Personnel files contain employment contracts/job description.
SURV_HOSP_183-Personnel files contain the qualifications of the staff member.
SURV_HOSP_185-Personnel files contain details of staff evaluations.
SURV_HOSP_187-Personnel files contain the work history of the staff member.
SURV_HOSP_189-Personnel files contain a record of in-service education attended by the staff member.
SURV_HOSP_191-Only authorised persons have access to personnel records.
SURV_HOSP_196-There are documented processes for staffing the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_198-The processes include personnel recruitment.
SURV_HOSP_200-The processes include numbers and categories of personnel required.
SURV_HOSP_202-The processes include desired education, qualifications, skills and knowledge of required personnel.
SURV_HOSP_204-The processes include assignment and reassignment of personnel.
SURV_HOSP_206-The processes include personal development of the personnel.
SURV_HOSP_208-The processes include staff retention.
SURV_HOSP_213-Personnel employed by the organisation have written job descriptions and performance agreements, which define their responsibilities.
SURV_HOSP_215-Job descriptions and performance agreements are reviewed according to organisational policy.
SURV_HOSP_217-Each staff member accepts their job description and performance agreement by signing it.
SURV_HOSP_221-Key performance areas for each staff member are identified in their job descriptions and performance agreements.
SURV_HOSP_223-There is at least one documented evaluation of each staff member each year or more frequently as defined by organisational policy.
SURV_HOSP_225-New staff members are evaluated as defined by organisational policy.
SURV_HOSP_229-Designated personnel are educated/trained in labour law legislation and related processes.
SURV_HOSP_231-There are mutually agreed processes for the satisfactory conduct of industrial relations activities, which meet the requirements of current legislation.
SURV_HOSP_233-Disciplinary procedures are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_235-Grievance procedures are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_237-Dispute and appeal procedures are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_239-There are recognition agreements with trade unions and/or health professional organisations, where applicable.
SURV_HOSP_244-There are documented programmes for staff orientation to the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_246-New staff members are orientated to the organisation within a time frame determined by organisational policy.
SURV_HOSP_248-Departmental and service managers implement orientation programmes for departmental and service personnel.
SURV_HOSP_250-Contract workers and/or volunteers are orientated to the organisation, their job responsibilities and their specific assignments.
SURV_HOSP_254-The organisation has a coordinated plan for in-service training and development.
SURV_HOSP_256-Department and service managers have established in-service training and development programmes relevant to departmental and service personnel.
SURV_HOSP_258-The organisation uses various sources of data and information to identify the in-service training and development needs of the personnel.
SURV_HOSP_260-The organisation provides on-going in-service training and development for its personnel.
SURV_HOSP_262-Staff competencies, where relevant, are assessed and recorded after in-service training and development.

SURV_HOSP_266-There is a continuing educational strategy developed by the management of the organisation for all professional personnel in the organisation to fulfil the requirements for continued registration with the relevant pr
SURV_HOSP_268-There is a development strategy for the organisation to ensure that managers receive the training required to fulfil their responsibilities.
SURV_HOSP_270-The personnel are informed of opportunities to participate in advanced education, training, research and other experiences.
SURV_HOSP_272-The organisation supports staff participation in such opportunities.
SURV_HOSP_274-The organisation supports the need for relevant postgraduate qualifications.
SURV_HOSP_279-Those permitted by law, regulation and the organisation to provide patient care without supervision are identified.
SURV_HOSP_281-The registration, education, training and experience of these individuals are documented.
SURV_HOSP_283-Such information is verified from the original sources.
SURV_HOSP_285-The personnel record contains copies of any required registration certificate(s).
SURV_HOSP_287-There is a process to review the personnel records annually.

SURV_HOSP_289-The organisation has a process to ensure that nurses and other professionals who are not employees of the organisation, but provide services to the organisation's patients, have valid credentials.
SURV_HOSP_291-The services to be provided are made known to appropriate individuals and units of the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_296-There are formalised quality improvement processes for the service that have been developed and agreed upon by the personnel of the service.
SURV_HOSP_298-Indicators of performance are identified to evaluate the quality of the service.
SURV_HOSP_300-The quality improvement cycle includes the monitoring and evaluation of the standards set and the remedial action implemented.
SURV_HOSP_302-The performance of individual staff members is reviewed when indicated by the findings of quality improvement activities.
SURV_HOSP_304-The performance of individual staff members is reviewed periodically, as established by the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_310-A designated financial manager is responsible for the implementation and maintenance of the financial strategy.
SURV_HOSP_312-The financial manager ensures that policies and procedures are available to guide the staff and that they are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_314-There is a mechanism for allowing the personnel to participate in the development and management of budgets (e.g. cost centres).
SURV_HOSP_316-A monthly report is produced for the organisation's management, setting out the financial position to date.
SURV_HOSP_318-There is a mechanism for establishing the reason for budget variation in either income or expenditure.
SURV_HOSP_320-Annual financial statements are produced at the end of the financial year.
SURV_HOSP_322-Internal/external audit reports and responses thereto are available.
SURV_HOSP_324-There is a capital asset register which is routinely maintained.
SURV_HOSP_326-There is a capital asset replacement programme.
SURV_HOSP_328-There is a mechanism to ensure that the level of debtors is kept to a minimum.
SURV_HOSP_333-A designated individual is responsible for the storage, maintenance and retrieval of health records.
SURV_HOSP_335-The health record manager ensures that policies and procedures are available to guide the personnel and that they are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_337-There is a written policy for addressing the privacy and confidentiality of information that is based on and consistent with laws and regulations.
SURV_HOSP_339-Originals of all reports by medical, nursing and other health professionals are filed in the records.
SURV_HOSP_341-A documented policy regarding records that are kept separately (e.g. psychiatric, social work, motor vehicle accidents) is implemented.
SURV_HOSP_343-There is a system that allows for the rapid retrieval and distribution of health records.
SURV_HOSP_345-There is a communication system for the request of health records.
SURV_HOSP_347-The organisation implements a policy regarding patients having access to their health information and the process for gaining access when permitted.
SURV_HOSP_349-There is an effective monitoring system (e.g. by using tracer cards) whereby records can be traced within the facility at all times.
SURV_HOSP_351-The filing system allows for incorrectly filed records to be easily identified (e.g. by using colour coding).
SURV_HOSP_353-There is provision for authorised access to health records at all times.
SURV_HOSP_355-Where electronic health records are used, there is a system to protect the integrity of the records.
SURV_HOSP_357-All storage areas for health records are secure against unauthorised entry.
SURV_HOSP_359-The organisation's policy on the retention of health records and other data and information is implemented.
SURV_HOSP_361-Policies and procedures for health record destruction, specifying the criteria for selection and the method of destruction of records, are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_366-An individual is designated to control the ordering, storage, distribution and control of equipment and supplies used in the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_368-The provisioning manager ensures that policies and procedures are available to guide the personnel and that they are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_370-A record is kept of goods received and goods issued.
SURV_HOSP_372-Records are audited.
SURV_HOSP_374-All losses are investigated, reported and recorded.
SURV_HOSP_376-There is an inventory of all goods stored.
SURV_HOSP_382-Hazardous and flammable materials are stored in accordance with relevant regulations.
SURV_HOSP_380-Secure storage facilities are available.
SURV_HOSP_384-Separate designated storage areas for a) receiving and b) unpacking incoming goods are provided.
SURV_HOSP_386-Arrangements, including alarm systems and door access controls, are used to provide security.
SURV_HOSP_388-There is adequate storage space to enable the retrieval and removal of equipment and supplies, when needed.
SURV_HOSP_393-A specific manager is identified for the control, use and maintenance of vehicles.
SURV_HOSP_395-The need to use transport is established by the management of the organisation in consultation with the users of vehicles and is reviewed annually.
SURV_HOSP_397-There is a system for monitoring the use of vehicles (e.g. permission, records).
SURV_HOSP_399-There is a system for booking vehicles in advance.
SURV_HOSP_401-There is a control system for mileage travelled.
SURV_HOSP_403-There is a vehicle maintenance plan.
SURV_HOSP_405-There is proof of vehicle maintenance.
SURV_HOSP_407-There is proof of current licensing of vehicles.
SURV_HOSP_409-Drivers of vehicles are suitably licensed.
SURV_HOSP_413-The organisation has a written response and deployment plan including the identification of response areas and availability of response units.
SURV_HOSP_415-Response time standards are monitored against national laws, regulations, policies or guidelines.
SURV_HOSP_417-The organisation designs and implements processes to provide coordination of services among other organisations and agencies in the community.
SURV_HOSP_419-The individuals who provide patient care in the ambulance services have the required training and experience.
SURV_HOSP_421-The organisation plans and implements processes for inspecting, testing and maintaining equipment and supplies.
SURV_HOSP_423-The organisation maintains its medical transport/ambulance vehicles to reduce risk and promote safety.
SURV_HOSP_425-Medical transport/ambulance vehicles are clean.
SURV_HOSP_431-Information on services, hours of operation and processes to obtain care are provided to agencies and referral sources in the community, and to the population served.
SURV_HOSP_433-Directional signage to the organisation is clearly visible from all main access roads.
SURV_HOSP_435-The name of the organisation and the services provided are clearly indicated on the site.
SURV_HOSP_437-Adequate parking is made available for patients and visitors.
SURV_HOSP_439-Directional signage within the organisation includes the most commonly used local languages and relevant symbols.
SURV_HOSP_441-There are areas for staff to obtain and give confidential information in privacy.
SURV_HOSP_443-Screening is initiated at the point of first contact with the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_445-The screening assessment leads to an understanding of the type of preventive, palliative, curative and rehabilitative services needed by the patient.
SURV_HOSP_447-The suitability of the patient for admission is based on the results of the screening, in accordance with the mission and resources of the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_449-Patients are accepted only if the organisation has the ability to provide the necessary services and settings for care.
SURV_HOSP_454-The organisation has identified the barriers in its patient population.
SURV_HOSP_456-There is a process to limit the impact of barriers on the delivery of services.
SURV_HOSP_458-Parking is made available close to the building entrance for physically challenged people.
SURV_HOSP_460-There is wheelchair access to and within the building.
SURV_HOSP_462-Ramps and stairs include safety features such as rails.
SURV_HOSP_466-Policies and procedures are used to standardise the outpatient registration process.
SURV_HOSP_468-Policies and procedures are used to standardise the inpatient admitting process.
SURV_HOSP_470-The policies and procedures address the admission of emergency patients to inpatient units.
SURV_HOSP_472-Patients with emergency or immediate needs are prioritised according to established criteria for assessment and intervention.
SURV_HOSP_474-Policies and procedures that address the holding of patients for observation are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_476-Policies and procedures that address the management of patients when bed space is not available in the desired service or unit or elsewhere in the facility are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_478-Policies and procedures for the management of patients deceased prior to arrival are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_482-There is a process to provide patient/family with information at admission.
SURV_HOSP_484-The process includes information on the proposed care and the expected results of care.
SURV_HOSP_486-The process includes information on any expected costs to the patient or family.
SURV_HOSP_488-Patients receive sufficient information to make knowledgeable decisions.
SURV_HOSP_492-The organisation has established entry and/or transfer criteria for its intensive and specialised units, including research and other programmes to meet special patient needs.
SURV_HOSP_494-The criteria are physiologic-based where possible and appropriate.
SURV_HOSP_496-Appropriate individuals are involved in developing the criteria.
SURV_HOSP_498-Personnel are trained to apply the criteria.
SURV_HOSP_500-Patients transferred or admitted to intensive and specialised units/services meet the criteria, as documented in the patient's record.
SURV_HOSP_502-Patients who no longer meet the criteria to remain in the unit are transferred or discharged.
SURV_HOSP_508-The leaders of the organisation work collaboratively to protect and advance patient and family rights through an established framework.
SURV_HOSP_510-Patient and family rights are identified and documented in accordance with relevant and current laws, charters and regulations.
SURV_HOSP_512-Policies and procedures that guide and support patient and family rights in the organisation are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_514-Personnel are trained on the policies and procedures and their participative role in the implementation of patients' rights.
SURV_HOSP_516-Each patient is given information about his/her rights in a language that he or she can understand.
SURV_HOSP_518-Processes ensure that care is considerate and respectful of the patient's personal values and beliefs.
SURV_HOSP_523-The patient's need for privacy is protected during all examinations, procedures and treatments.
SURV_HOSP_525-The patient's need for privacy is protected when providing personal information.
SURV_HOSP_527-The organisation respects patient health information as confidential.
SURV_HOSP_529-Patients receive information on the organisation's level of responsibility for patients' possessions.
SURV_HOSP_533-The organisation has a process to protect patients from assault.
SURV_HOSP_535-Remote or isolated areas of the hospital are monitored.
SURV_HOSP_537-The organisation implements processes to ensure that patients are not subjected to abuse from healthcare professionals.
SURV_HOSP_542-The organisation plans education consistent with its mission, services and patient population.
SURV_HOSP_544-There is an appropriate structure or mechanism for education throughout the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_549-Patients and families are informed about their rights to refuse or discontinue treatment.
SURV_HOSP_551-Patients are informed about the consequences of such decisions.
SURV_HOSP_555-The organisation has identified its position on withholding resuscitative services and forgoing or withdrawing life-sustaining treatments.
SURV_HOSP_557-The organisation's position conforms to its community's religious and cultural norms and to any legal or regulatory requirements.
SURV_HOSP_559-Policies and procedures that guide the processes for patients to make their decisions known to the organisation and for modifying decisions during the course of care are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_561-Policies and procedures that guide the organisation's response to patient decisions are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_563-The organisation guides health professionals on the ethical and legal issues in carrying out such patient wishes.
SURV_HOSP_568-There is a mechanism to allow for the hearing of complaints and to act upon them.
SURV_HOSP_570-Patients are aware of their right to voice complaints and the processes by which to do so.
SURV_HOSP_572-Complaints are reviewed according to the organisation's mechanism.
SURV_HOSP_577-Policies and procedures that guide the staff in the process of gaining informed consent are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_579-High risk and problem-prone procedures are identified and listed as requiring special consent.
SURV_HOSP_581-A note is made in the patient's record when any individual, other than the patient, grants consent.
SURV_HOSP_583-General consent/acknowledgement of admission requirements is obtained when patients enter the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_585-Patients and families acknowledge the scope of such general consent/admission requirements.
SURV_HOSP_591-Information systems are developed and implemented in the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_593-Those who provide clinical and managerial services identify their information needs.
SURV_HOSP_595-Clinical and managerial personnel participate in information technology decisions.
SURV_HOSP_599-Confidentiality of data and information is maintained.
SURV_HOSP_601-Security and integrity of data and information is maintained.
SURV_HOSP_606-Sufficient personnel support the implementation.
SURV_HOSP_608-Decision-makers and others are provided with appropriate training in the principles of information management.
SURV_HOSP_610-Required technology and other resources support the implementation.
SURV_HOSP_614-A designated individual supervises the management of ICT equipment in the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_616-Policies and procedures that guide the management of ICT equipment are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_618-All desktop and server computers are attached to an uninterrupted power supply (UPS) with surge protection.
SURV_HOSP_620-Records are kept of the maintenance of ICT equipment.
SURV_HOSP_622-Where technical ICT support is not available at facility level, an arrangement is in place to obtain such support from outside.
SURV_HOSP_624-There is a documented procedure known to personnel for reporting defects in ICT equipment during and after normal working hours.
SURV_HOSP_629-The organisation has a process to aggregate data.
SURV_HOSP_631-Clinical and managerial data and information are integrated as needed to support decision-making.
SURV_HOSP_633-Aggregated data and information are used to support patient care.
SURV_HOSP_635-Aggregated data and information are used to support management of the organisation.
SURV_HOSP_637-Aggregated data and information are used to support the quality management programmes.
SURV_HOSP_641-The organisation has a process to participate in or use information from external databases.
SURV_HOSP_643-Data or information is contributed to external databases as required by law or regulation, where applicable.
SURV_HOSP_645-The organisation compares its performance with that of other similar organisations using external reference databases.

SURV_HOSP_651-There are documented risk management processes for the identification of all risks (physical, environmental, medico-legal, operational, etc) relating to organisational processes and systems, staff, patients, visitors
SURV_HOSP_653-Risk management processes include documented plans and actions to eliminate or reduce the identified risks.
SURV_HOSP_655-Risk management processes include on-going documented monitoring of risks.
SURV_HOSP_657-Management and leaders ensure the development and implementation of documented policies and procedures for risk management processes and activities.
SURV_HOSP_659-On-going in-service training of all staff in these policies, procedures and risk management principles, including reporting of adverse events, is documented.
SURV_HOSP_661-One or more qualified and/or skilled and/or experienced individuals supervise the implementation of the risk management system.
SURV_HOSP_663-Analysed data, including adverse events and near misses, are used to monitor the effectiveness of the risk management system.
SURV_HOSP_665-Risk management systems are reviewed whenever there are changes in organisational systems and processes or physical facilities.
SURV_HOSP_670-Policies and/or procedures that address the accuracy of patient identification are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_672-The policies and/or procedures require the use of two patient identifiers, not including the use of the patient's room number or locations.
SURV_HOSP_674-Patients are identified before administering medications, blood or blood products.
SURV_HOSP_676-Patients are identified before taking blood and other specimens for clinical testing.
SURV_HOSP_678-Patients are identified before providing treatments and procedures.
SURV_HOSP_682-Policies and/or procedures that address the accuracy of verbal and telephone orders are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_684-The complete verbal and telephone order or test result is written down by the receiver of the order or test result, who signs as having done so.
SURV_HOSP_686-The complete verbal and telephone order or test result is read back by a second person, who signs as having done so.
SURV_HOSP_688-The order or test result is confirmed by the individual who gave the order or test result by signing the relevant document as per organisation policy.
SURV_HOSP_692-Policies and/or procedures that address the location, labelling and storage of concentrated electrolytes are implemented.

SURV_HOSP_694-Concentrated electrolytes are not present in patient care units unless clinically necessary and actions are taken to prevent inadvertent administration in those areas where permitted by policy.
SURV_HOSP_698-Policies and/or procedures that establish uniform processes to ensure the identification of the correct site, correct procedure and correct patient are implemented.
SURV_HOSP_700-The organisation uses a clearly understood mark for surgical site identification and involves the patient in the marking process.
SURV_HOSP_702-The organisation uses a process to verify that all documents and equipment needed to perform the marking are on hand, correct and functional.
